findings.title: Freshly initialized markets are immediately borrowable at a zero exchange rate

best_agent_match.title: Freshly initialized markets can be drained because `exchangeRate` starts at zero

findings.title: Solvency-critical actions trust arbitrarily stale cached oracle prices

best_agent_match.title: Borrowing, collateral removal, and liquidation all rely on arbitrarily stale cached oracle data

findings.title: `cook()` max-rate protection is inverted and only passes above the caller's ceiling

best_agent_match.title: The `cook` exchange-rate upper bound is inverted, so slippage protection only passes at worse-than-allowed prices

findings.title: Permissionless `cook()` calls can sweep stray ETH and directly transferred tokens from the Cauldron

best_agent_match.title: Anyone can steal ETH or tokens stranded on the Cauldron through unrestricted `cook` calls

rejected_candidates.title: Missing access control on init function allows setting malicious oracle
rejected_candidates.reason: `init()` is the intended clone initializer invoked during `BentoBox.deploy`; no concrete path was shown to hijack already deployed funded markets, and initializing the master contract itself does not compromise existing clones.

rejected_candidates.title: Reentrancy vulnerability in liquidate function via arbitrary swapper
rejected_candidates.reason: The swapper callback occurs before the final MIM repayment transfer, and any failure to repay reverts the entire liquidation; no concrete reentrant path to steal collateral or corrupt accounting was validated.

rejected_candidates.title: No slippage protection in liquidation swap
rejected_candidates.reason: Open liquidation intentionally lets the caller choose `to` and `swapper`; the liquidator bears that execution risk, so this is not a protocol vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: Oracle price not validated for reasonableness
rejected_candidates.reason: This is a generic oracle-trust observation rather than a distinct code flaw; the concrete reportable oracle issues here are the stale/zero cached-rate behaviors already captured separately.

rejected_candidates.title: Open liquidation allows front-running and MEV extraction
rejected_candidates.reason: Permissionless liquidation and MEV competition are expected market mechanics, not a code-level vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: Potential integer overflow in accrue interest calculation
rejected_candidates.reason: The arithmetic is checked, and the described overflow requires extreme/unrealistic values or time horizons, making it non-practical as a protocol risk.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing event for critical reduceSupply function
rejected_candidates.reason: Informational only; it does not create realistic protocol-level harm.

rejected_candidates.title: No access control on setFeeTo function
rejected_candidates.reason: `setFeeTo()` is protected by `onlyOwner`; setting a poor recipient is an authorized governance choice, not missing access control.
